name: google-bigquery
description: Explore BigQuery datasets and tables and run SQL queries against your Google Cloud data warehouse

tools.name: List Projects
tools.description: List the Google Cloud projects the connected user can access, so agents can discover the projectId every other tool requires

maxResults.description: Maximum number of projects to return per page (up to 50 per page)

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

tools.name: List Datasets
tools.description: List all BigQuery datasets in a Google Cloud project

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID that owns the datasets

maxResults.description: Maximum number of datasets to return per page

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

tools.name: List Tables
tools.description: List tables and views in a BigQuery dataset

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID that owns the dataset

datasetId.description: Dataset ID to list tables from

maxResults.description: Maximum number of tables to return per page

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

tools.name: Get Table Schema
tools.description: Get a table's metadata including its full column schema, row count, and size

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID that owns the table

selectedFields.description: Comma-separated list of table schema fields to return; returns all fields if unset

tools.name: Preview Table Data
tools.description: Read sample rows directly from a table without running a query - no bytes are billed, ideal for inspecting data before writing SQL

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID that owns the table

tableId.description: Table ID to read rows from

maxResults.description: Maximum number of rows to return

startIndex.description: Zero-based index of the starting row to read

selectedFields.description: Comma-separated subset of fields to return, supports nested sub-fields, e.g. a,e.d.f

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

tools.name: Run SQL Query
tools.description: Run a read/SELECT GoogleSQL query and return the first page of results. With the default read-only scope only SELECT queries work; DML statements (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) require the full https://www.googleapis.com/auth/bigquery scope.

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID to bill the query against

query.description: GoogleSQL SELECT query, e.g. SELECT name FROM `project.dataset.table` LIMIT 10

useLegacySql.description: Set false to use GoogleSQL (standard SQL); the API defaults to legacy SQL when omitted

maxResults.description: Maximum number of result rows to return in the first page

timeoutMs.description: How long (ms) to wait for the query to complete before returning with jobComplete=false

dryRun.description: If true, validate the query and estimate bytes processed without running it

location.description: Geographic location where the query should run, e.g. US or EU

tools.name: Get Query Results
tools.description: Fetch additional pages of results from a previously started query job, using the jobId and pageToken returned by Run SQL Query

projectId.description: Google Cloud project ID that the query job ran in

jobId.description: Job ID returned in jobReference.jobId by Run SQL Query

maxResults.description: Maximum number of result rows to return per page

pageToken.description: Page token from a previous response for pagination

startIndex.description: Zero-based index of the starting row to read

timeoutMs.description: How long (ms) to wait for the job to complete before returning

location.description: Geographic location of the job, e.g. US or EU

prompts.title: Explore my data warehouse
prompts.prompt: List the datasets and tables in my BigQuery project and summarize what data is available.

prompts.title: Answer a question with SQL
prompts.prompt: Write and run a BigQuery SQL query to answer a question about my data, then explain the results.

prompts.title: Describe a table
prompts.prompt: Show me the schema of a BigQuery table and suggest useful queries against it.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google BigQuery setup

steps.title: Create or reuse a Google Cloud project
steps.description: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and create a project (or reuse the one that already hosts your Google OAuth app). Note its project ID — every BigQuery call takes a projectId parameter (the same value you would put in a GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT env var). Without billing enabled you can still use the free BigQuery sandbox to test queries against public datasets.

steps.title: Enable the BigQuery API
steps.description: Open https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/bigquery.googleapis.com, select your project, and click Enable.

steps.title: Configure the OAuth consent screen and client
steps.description: Under APIs & Services > Credentials, create (or reuse) an OAuth 2.0 Client ID of type Web application. Add your app's redirect URI ending in /api/auth/google-bigquery/callback. The same Google OAuth client can be shared across all Google connectors.

steps.title: Set environment variables
steps.description: Set GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET from the OAuth client you created. These are shared with other Google integrations.

notes: The default scope is read-only (cloud-platform.read-only): listing datasets/tables and SELECT queries work, but DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) requires granting the optional full bigquery scope.
There is no project-wide env var: pass the Google Cloud project ID as the projectId parameter on every call (treat it like GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT).
Queries are billed to the projectId you pass; use dryRun to estimate bytes processed before running expensive queries.
The query API defaults to legacy SQL — keep useLegacySql set to false to write GoogleSQL.
